summary: Recommended production-ready AI tools and services for Mason to enhance self-monitoring, code quality, and AI infrastructure integration on Windows.

categories.label: Core self-ops & health
categories.description: Tools that help Mason observe, log, and heal himself.

categories.label: Code review & refactoring
categories.description: Tools that help Mason analyze and upgrade his own scripts.

categories.label: AI infrastructure
categories.description: Tools and services for model usage, evaluation, and orchestration.

tools.name: Prometheus + Node Exporter
tools.description: Prometheus is a widely used open-source monitoring system that can scrape metrics from Node Exporter running on Windows. This setup helps Mason monitor system health, resource usage, and custom application metrics for self-observation and alerting.
tools.integration_idea: Install Node Exporter on Windows and run Prometheus server locally or remotely. Mason can query Prometheus HTTP API from PowerShell to fetch health metrics and trigger alerts or self-healing scripts.
tools.notes: Requires running a local or networked Prometheus server. Node Exporter must be configured to expose relevant metrics. Safe and read-only monitoring.

tools.name: OpenAI CLI
tools.description: Official OpenAI CLI tool to interact with OpenAI models non-interactively from Windows PowerShell. Useful for planning, code review, and safe external AI model usage.
tools.integration_idea: Install OpenAI CLI via npm or direct executable. Mason can call it from PowerShell scripts to send prompts for code analysis, refactoring suggestions, or planning tasks.
tools.notes: Requires OpenAI API key and internet access. Use with rate limits and secure key storage to maintain safety.

tools.name: SonarScanner CLI
tools.description: SonarScanner is a CLI tool to analyze code quality, detect bugs, code smells, and security vulnerabilities. It integrates with SonarQube or SonarCloud for detailed reports, helping Mason improve his own codebase.
tools.integration_idea: Install SonarScanner on Windows and configure it to scan Mason's code directories. Run scans from PowerShell and upload results to SonarQube/SonarCloud for review.
tools.notes: Requires setup of SonarQube server or SonarCloud account. Scans are local and safe; no code is sent externally unless using SonarCloud.

tools.name: GitHub CLI (gh)
tools.description: GitHub CLI enables automation of code review workflows, pull requests, and issue management from the command line. Mason can automate code refactoring reviews and integrate AI suggestions into GitHub workflows.
tools.integration_idea: Install GitHub CLI on Windows and authenticate. Mason can script PR creation, commenting, and merging from PowerShell to streamline code quality processes.
tools.notes: Requires GitHub account and repository access. Safe for personal PC usage with proper token management.

tools.name: LangChain Python Library
tools.description: LangChain is a Python library to build AI applications with external model integrations, prompt management, and chaining. Mason can use it to orchestrate AI calls safely and modularly for planning and code review.
tools.integration_idea: Install Python and LangChain on Windows. Mason can run Python scripts from PowerShell that use LangChain to call OpenAI or other models with controlled prompts and outputs.
tools.notes: Requires Python environment setup and API keys for external models. Code execution should be sandboxed and monitored for safety.

tools.name: Logrotate for Windows (e.g., logrotate-win)
tools.description: Logrotate-win is a Windows port of the popular log rotation tool. It helps Mason manage log file sizes and retention to prevent disk overuse and maintain clean logs for monitoring.
tools.integration_idea: Install logrotate-win and configure rotation policies for Mason's log directories. Run logrotate commands from PowerShell on schedule or trigger.
tools.notes: Ensure configuration files are correct to avoid accidental log deletion. Safe and lightweight.

tools.name: Microsoft Application Insights CLI
tools.description: Application Insights CLI allows sending telemetry and logs to Azure Application Insights for advanced monitoring and alerting. Mason can use it to centralize logs and monitor health remotely.
tools.integration_idea: Install Azure CLI and Application Insights extension. Mason can send custom telemetry events from PowerShell scripts to Application Insights.
tools.notes: Requires Azure subscription and internet access. Data privacy and cost considerations apply.
